item.title: "I apologized to the group," he said following the …
item.description: It also came after he addressed his teammates in the locker room at halftime. "I apologized to the group," he said following the series-evening win. "I set the tone in Game 3, getting downhill in transition, and I don't think I did that one time in the first half. And for me, that's the catalyst. It's not always just the scoring, right? Like I was able to get into the paint and score. Trying to just at least create confusion, trying to set the tone early in the offense. I didn't do that in the f

item.title: JB Bickerstaff criticizes officiating: ‘Ever since we came to Cleveland, the whistle has changed’
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff on the 34-12 foul disparity: “It’s unacceptable. It is. We didn’t do enough obviously to help ourselves and I’ll start there, but ever since we came to Cleveland the whistle has changed.”

item.title: Donovan Mitchell demolishes Pistons in the second half to even series
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs beat the Pistons 112-103 in a game that wasn't as close as the final score. Donovan Mitchell had one of his best games as a member of the Cavs, finishing with 43 points -- 39 of them in the 2nd half. Harden had 24 and hit five 3s. Series is tied at 2-2.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell ties Sleepy Floyd’s record for most points in a playoff half
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Most points in a playoff half ... 39 - Donovan Mitchell (2nd), tonight; 39 - Sleepy Floyd (2nd), 1987; 38 - Charles Barkley (1st), 1994; 38 - Kevin Durant (1st), 2019

item.title: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth …
item.description: ESPN Insight: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth 40-point playoff game with the Cavaliers, passing Kyrie Irving for second-most in franchise history . Only LeBron James (24) has more.

item.title: The @cavs' 24-0 run was the longest in an @NBA playoff …
item.description: Cavs Notes: The @cavs' 24-0 run was the longest in an @NBA playoff game since Minnesota scored 24 straight on May 16, 2024 against DEN. It was the longest run in a playoff game by Cleveland since 1997-98. The team’s previous long over that span was 19 straight on May 16, 2008 vs. BOS.

item.title: Congrats to @James Harden of the @cavs for moving up …
item.description: NBA: Congrats to @James Harden of the @cavs for moving up to 7th on the all-time playoff STEALS list!

item.description: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back spasms) as questionable for Monday's Game 4 against Detroit.

item.title: Atkinson and Blair played on the same college …
item.description: But Atkinson, the Cavaliers head coach, largely steered clear. In the fourth quarter with the game on the line, Blair called a loose-ball foul on the Cavs and Atkinson hated the call, but rather than scream or dart toward the official, Atkinson just sort of stood near his bench, staring at Blair and shaking his head. There’s history here, and not the usual kind between a coach and a referee. A lifetime ago, Atkinson and Blair played on the same college basketball team. That and $6.85 would be en

item.title: “There is no, I’m not going to say anything to Curtis …
item.description: “You know, listen, there’s no, like, friends, right? We’re competing,” Atkinson told The Athletic in a phone call Saturday after his team’s 116-109 Game 3 win. “There is no, I’m not going to say anything to Curtis because we went to school together. There’s none of that. I would say it’s like super professional, our interactions. But we both know we have this shared history.”

item.title: And yes, from 1988 to 1990, they were teammates for …
item.description: Atkinson, 58, has been on an NBA sideline as an assistant or head coach since 2008. Blair, 55, is in his 18th season as an NBA official, which means they broke into the league at roughly the same time. And yes, from 1988 to 1990, they were teammates for the “giant killer” Richmond Spiders teams of the era, forming a tantalizing backcourt when Atkinson was a senior and Blair a sophomore and the two led the team in scoring.

item.title: “We’re never like, ‘Hey, Curt, remember when you hit …
item.description: Atkinson said he and Blair have never sat down over coffee or a bottle of red to rehash the past, how they got to where they are now, or how to navigate the two. Those who knew them when say neither of them brings it up in phone conversations, either. Kenny coaches. Curtis refs. “We’re never like, ‘Hey, Curt, remember when you hit the buzzer-beater against American University to put us in the finals of the CAA tournament?’ We don’t reminisce,” Atkinson said. “We’re not joking about (their Richmo

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. Tobias Harris’ late contest on James Harden’s 3-point dagger was clean, not considered a foul. “Harris jumps to contest Harden's shot, but would otherwise avoid making contact if not for Harden extending forward.”

item.title: “Magic, Michael, Kareem, Kobe had killer instincts. …
item.description: Run It Back: “Magic, Michael, Kareem, Kobe had killer instincts. ... I love LeBron, but at times he doesn't bring it the way we want him to bring it, like James Harden." - Tim Hardaway Sr.

item.title: “They’re getting more calls than we are.”
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. when asked how the whistle has changed after Kenny Atkinson’s comments: “They’re getting more calls than we are.”

item.title: “With Cade, you cannot give him the same look. He’s …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Kenny Atkinson on defending Cade: “With Cade, you cannot give him the same look. He’s like a great quarterback, you give the same look to him he’s gonna figure it out. Keep mixing and matching different coverages, different matchups.

item.title: “What’s hard to do is stop momentum. … It shifts back …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Kenny Atkinson on the Cavs having momentum after winning the last two: “What’s hard to do is stop momentum. … It shifts back and forth, but I do like that we’ve caught this momentum and energy. We’ve gotta bring it forward, we’ve gotta carry it over to the road.”

item.title: Cade Cunningham had a game-high 39 points on 6/10 from …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cavs hold on to beat the Pistons 117-113 after outscoring them 14-10 in overtime. Cleveland takes a 3-2 series lead. Cade Cunningham had a game-high 39 points on 6/10 from distance, 9 assists, 7 boards and 2 steals.

item.title: Pistons led by 9 with 2:37 left in the 4th quarter
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Pistons led by 9 with 2:37 left in the 4th quarter. They were outscored 23-10 the through the end of the 4th & OT, shooting 4-for-17 from the field.

item.title: Cade Cunningham: 'We all saw the play, I think it’s pretty clear it was a foul'
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cade Cunningham on the final play of regulation: “We all saw the play, I think it’s pretty clear it was a foul. (Ausar) has the ball in his possession, he’s running forward, he gets tripped up. It’s a foul, it’s been a foul the whole rest of the game. Wasn’t a foul that time.”

item.title: On the late no-call against Ausar: “He fouled Ausar. …
item.description: ?Omari Sankofa II?: JB: “They started doubling and getting the ball out of Cade’s hands. We’ll find ways to fix it. We’ll be better.” On the late no-call against Ausar: “He fouled Ausar. It’s clear. He trips him when he’s going for a loose ball. End of game situation, that’s tough.”

item.title: Cleveland outscored Detroit 23-10, shot 50% to the …
item.description: ?Kurt Helin?: In the final 3 minutes and OT, Cleveland outscored Detroit 23-10, shot 50% to the Pistons 23.5%, was 2-of-3 from 3 while Detroit was 0-of-5, and Mitchell was 4-of-5 with 9 points, while Cunningham was 1-of-4 for 2 points.

item.title: The former NBA Most Valuable Player became just the …
item.description: The former NBA Most Valuable Player became just the fourth player in the history of the Association to score at least 4,000 points, collect 1,000 rebounds and dish out 1,000 dimes in the playoffs. Two of the players to have done it are in the Basketball Hall of Fame, while the other is a lock to be inducted into Springfield.

item.title: “Just sticking with it. Every single minute, every …
item.description: Evan Mobley emphasized composure and collective execution after the Cleveland Cavaliers escaped with a 117-113 overtime win over the Detroit Pistons in Game 5 of the Eastern Conference semifinals. “Just sticking with it. Every single minute, every single second counts at the end of games like this and I think we stuck with it till the very end,” Mobley said, describing the mindset behind Cleveland’s comeback from a nine-point deficit in the final minutes of regulation. The Cavaliers turned a 103

item.title: Pistons got Evan Mobley looking like Scar
item.description: ?Spencer Davies?: Pistons got Evan Mobley looking like Scar.

item.title: Max Strus: "I look better? You're saying I look …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Max Strus: "I look better? You're saying I look bigger?" "I do. I think you look like the Pistons. They got a bunch of big guys. I think you look like them" Max: "I'll take that as a compliment" ??

item.title: "That's a pretty man. He's a good looking man. I would …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Chuck on Max Strus: "That's a pretty man. He's a good looking man. I would not look him dead in the eyes. I made that mistake with Tom Brady one time. He's a better shooter than Tom Brady" ??

item.title: Darryn Peterson breaks down what he's learning from Devin Booker, Anthony Edwards, SGA, Donovan Mitchell and Dame Lillard
item.description: Q. Some film you're watching of guys that you're trying to emulate at the next level? Darryn Peterson: Devin Booker, Anthony Edwards, Shai,, Donovan Mitchell, and Dame. Q. What are you taking from them particularly? Peterson: Just ways that they score. They all score in different ways. Let's see how they take space and most of them are getting guarded super hard during their game. How they get easy shots, how they get to free throw line.

item.title: Some tipoff scenarios for Game 1 of ECF if it ends up …
item.description: Ian Begley: Some tipoff scenarios for Game 1 of ECF if it ends up being played on Sunday:

item.title: Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. the finalists for Social Justice Champion award
item.description: NBA: As we honor the sixth year of the NBA Social Justice Champion award, join us in recognizing Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. as the finalists for the 2025-26 season. This award honors NBA Hall of Famer Kareem Abdul-Jabbar and his lifetime commitment to driving impactful change around social justice.

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Kevin Huerter, Caris LeVert will be available against Cavs
item.description: The Pistons have upgraded Duncan Robinson (low back soreness), Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) and Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) to available for Friday's Game 6 against Cleveland.

item.title: Bickerstaff: “Horse shit.”
item.description: Jackson Flickinger: J.B. Bickerstaff: “What did the two-minute report say?” Joe Vardon: “No foul.” Bickerstaff: “Horse shit.”

item.title: Cavs were +4 in 11 minutes with Donovan Mitchell OFF …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Cavs were +4 in 11 minutes with Donovan Mitchell OFF court in Game 6 tonight. They were -25 in his 37 minutes ON court. They lost the 8 minutes that Mitchell was on court without Harden by 16 points, shot 4-for-16 from the field in those minutes.

item.title: “That’s a great question.”
item.description: NBA on ESPN: “That’s a great question.” — James Harden on the Cavs not playing with the intensity needed to close out the series.

item.description: Hunter Patterson: Pistons beat the Cavs 115-94 to force another Game 7. Back to LCA we go.

item.title: James Harden is now 4-14 in Game 6s in his career, the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: James Harden is now 4-14 in Game 6s in his career, the second-worst record in Game 6 in NBA playoff history.

item.title: James Harden has tied the record for most games in a …
item.description: Kellan Olson: James Harden has tied the record for most games in a postseason with 7+ turnovers by doing it for the fourth time tonight, per @Stathead. He is the only player to do it in at least two different postseasons and this the third occurrence.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell is now 1-6 in Game 6s in his career.
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Donovan Mitchell is now 1-6 in Game 6s in his career. He hasn't won a Game 6 since April 2018!!

item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Cavs have given up 20+ points off turnovers in 8 games this postseason, tied for 3rd-most such games in one playoff run in the play-by-play era.

item.title: Draymond Green: 'Bob Myers has been to Daryl Morey almost what Michael Jordan was to Stockton and Malone'
item.description: Draymond Green: “Bob Myers has been to Daryl Morey almost what Michael Jordan was to Stockton and Malone. You’ve done great work. I mean, I’m not saying Daryl Morey is Stockton or Malone, but you’ll get the reference in a second. You’ve done some great work, right? You traded for James Harden when nobody believed, gave him a max contract, and then James Harden proceeded to become who he became. You built a team with the Houston Rockets that was a very good roster, built to beat the Golden State

item.title: James Harden will finish with more turnovers than made …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: James Harden will finish with more turnovers than made field goals for the 31st time in his playoff career & the 5th time this postseason.

item.title: J.B. Bickerstaff on James Harden: "He has an …
item.description: J.B. Bickerstaff on James Harden: "He has an unbelievable ability to manipulate what the rules are. We would play 1 on 1 when I coached in Houston and he would foul bait me."

item.title: "I can’t dwell on it," he told reporters after Game 6. …
item.description: Yet in the end, Mitchell isn't letting his horrific Game 6 affect him in approaching the must-win seventh game back in Detroit on Sunday night. "I can’t dwell on it," he told reporters after Game 6. "I missed shots tonight, and sure, some of them were probably tougher shots that I could have handled better. But you can say that about every game. I’m not focused on the fact that I missed shots. It’s more about the overall force and impact on the game. "I’ve been making those shots for most of thi

But then you also have guys who play the game very pure. I look at someone like Giannis. What they told him — and who knows if I’m supposed to be saying this or not — is that he doesn’t flop. Giannis is one of very few players in this league who doesn’t flail his body and flop and move around. So he actually does

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert, Kevin Huerter listed as questionable for Game 7
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Robinson, LeVert and Huerter all remain questionable for Game 7 tomorrow

item.title: Cavs expected to keep James Harden on new deal
item.description: James Harden wanted contract certainty after this season, and the Clippers made it clear at midseason that they hadn't decided whether to pick up his $42 million team option -- $13 million guaranteed -- for next season. When both sides showed their cards, Harden's representation got permission to find a team willing to commit to him. The Cavs were willing. Though a formal agreement is not allowed to be completed before the offseason, there is an understanding that the Cavs plan to work out a new

item.title: The Cavs will likely want to replace Donovan …
item.description: The Cavs will likely want to replace Donovan Mitchell's option season with a new contract in July, when they can add four years and up to $272 million. But Mitchell can, and probably should, wait until 2027 to sign a new contract. Then, with 10 years of service time, he can sign for five years and about $350 million, plus get some perks, such as a no-trade clause. That is a massive difference in guaranteed money for a player who would be in his mid-30s by the time the extra season kicked in.

item.title: Larry Nance Jr ruled out for Game 7 vs. Detroit
item.description: The Cavaliers have downgraded Larry Nance Jr (illness) as out for Sunday’s Game 7 against Detroit

item.title: Similar to Game 5 when they took eight busloads of …
item.description: Nick Camino: Dan Gilbert and the @cavs went BIG for tonight’s Game 7 in Detroit. Similar to Game 5 when they took eight busloads of season ticket holders up to Detroit for the game, today they’re bringing up 25 busloads of #Cavs fans, with all expenses covered for Game 7. Yes, TWENTY-FIVE!

item.title: I think he found something that he liked and that he …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. on whether Jalen Duren got rolling in Game 6 because Stew and Reed played early: “I’m not sure if it was the timing of those guys’ minutes. We were able to find more space for him to operate. … I think he found something that he liked and that he can replicate.”

item.title: Hope it works again tonight.”
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. on starting Daniss last game: “Even with Ausar in that lineup you’re seeing there are three guys who bring the ball up. So they can’t just hone in on where (Cade) is gonna be. We can move him around more freely, the point of attack changes. … Hope it works again tonight.”

item.title: It wasn’t this huge thing we had to overcome. It was …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB: “For us, the focus has always just been the moment and it’s just one game. That's the way we’ve treated these playoffs since being down 3-1 against Orlando. It wasn’t this huge thing we had to overcome. It was just, you had to win one game.”

item.title: The plan is to move Dean Wade out of the starting …
item.description: Sources tell cleveland.com that coach Kenny Atkinson is likely to make a starting lineup change for Game 7 of the Eastern Conference semifinals against the Detroit Pistons Sunday night. The plan is to move Dean Wade out of the starting lineup, swapping in battle-tested veteran Max Strus.

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert and Kevin Huerter are …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert and Kevin Huerter are all available for the Pistons tonight.

item.title: Kenny Atkinson on Cavs adjustments made: We were avoiding Ausar Thompson
item.description: MrBuckBuck: Kenny Atkinson on the Cleveland Cavaliers' adjustments: "The tactical adjustment we made was avoiding [Ausar] Thompson. We were just like, if he's near the ball, throw it to someone else." Via: https:// reddit.com/r/nba/comments /1tgauk0/kenny_atkinson_on_the_cleveland_cavaliers/

item.title: "That game sucked."
item.description: ESPN: "That game sucked." Cade Cunningham on the Pistons' Game 7 loss at home against the Cavs.

item.title: “Obviously a disappointing series right here. Fell …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cade Cunningham on this season: “Obviously a disappointing series right here. Fell short of doing enough of the right things to win the series. But we were the No. 1 seed for a reason.”

item.title: There was a great opportunity to move forward and to …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Cade on if this loss will fuel them: "A lot. There was a great opportunity to move forward and to continue to experience new things that we hadn't been exposed to. Fell short. We were right there but we fell short. That chip and that sting, we're going to feel for a while."

item.title: We didn't come out with the right disposition for a …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Robinson: "I don't want to be over-reactionary having not watched it ... we didn't come out with the right disposition for a Game 7 and that was apparent in all the areas we prided ourselves on this year, areas we thought we could compete with and win, we didn't win those."

item.title: Jarrett Allen said that #Cavs owner Dan Gilbert sent …
item.description: Danny Cunningham: Jarrett Allen said that #Cavs owner Dan Gilbert sent him a message of encouragement before Game 7.

item.title: “There’s Black History Month JA and Game 7 JA”

item.title: Cavs are 2nd team ever to win a Game 7 by 20+ points …
item.description: Josh Dubow: Cavs are 2nd team ever to win a Game 7 by 20+ points after losing Game 6 by 20+ points. The Magic did it in 1995 Eastern Conference final, losing Game 6 123-96 at Indiana and winning Game 7 105-81 at home

item.title: “It’s going to be a loud environment; we know that …
item.description: Ian Begley: “It’s going to be a loud environment; we know that their fans are incredible fans, crazy fans. But we know that we can do it. We came into an incredible arena like this (DET in G7) & took over the game. We just have to do it again” - Jarret Allen to @CassidyHubbarth on CLE @ NYK for Game 1 of ECF on Tuesday. Knicks have home-court advantage. ECF games will be played every other day. Long Island’s Kenny Atkinson, Westchester native/Mets fan Donovan Mitchell and Cavs have won 3 of 4. K

item.title: Cavs blowout Pistons in Game 7, will face Knicks in conference finals
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs dominate the Detroit Pistons 125-94 to advance to the Eastern Conference Finals. Donovan Mitchell was excellent, Jarrett Allen and Evan Mobley were too, and Sam Merrill was outstanding off the bench. As dominant of a performance as they've had this year.

item.title: Their deepest run since 2008 ends with an incredibly …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: FINAL: Cavaliers 125, Pistons 94. The Pistons have been eliminated from the playoffs. Their deepest run since 2008 ends with an incredibly flat, disappointing performance on their home floor. Lots to build off of this season, but this was a nightmare way for it to end.

item.title: Cavs had four players with over 20 points in Game 7 …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: Cavs had four players with over 20 points in Game 7 with Donovan Mitchell (26), Jarrett Allen (23), Sam Merrill (23) and Evan Mobley (21 points, 12 rebounds).

item.title: Donovan Mitchell just finished with no turnovers and …
item.description: Law Murray: Donovan Mitchell just finished with no turnovers and eight assists His fifth game of his postseason career with no turnovers... and the most assists of those five games.

item.title: Cavaliers have advance to the Eastern Conference …
item.description: Danny Cunningham: The Cavaliers have advance to the Eastern Conference Finals without LeBron James for the first time since 1992.

item.title: He said, ‘The key to this whole thing, the spark for …
item.description: Among the advice Dan Gilbert offered Kenny Atkinson, the coach said, was that Jarrett Allen would be a key to Game 7. “He said, ‘You know who the key to this whole thing is,’” Atkinson said. “I’m thinking (James) Harden, (Donovan) Mitchell. He said, ‘The key to this whole thing, the spark for this is Jarrett Allen.’ I said, ‘Really?’ And that sparked me to run the first play for him. … I went to my room like, man, I’ve got to run the first play for him.

item.title: The Cleveland faithful were chanting “Let’s go, Cavs” …
item.description: Gilbert’s plans worked to perfection. As previously mentioned, Allen controlled the game. He (and, to be fair, Evan Mobley) set the tone on the interior, and Mitchell was masterful with his 26 points, eight assists, and seven boards. Sam Merrill made five 3s off the bench. The Cavs blew the doors off. And so did their fans. With the rout fully underway late in the third quarter and Detroit fans not having left quite yet, the Cleveland faithful were chanting “Let’s go, Cavs” loud enough that it c

item.title: Cavaliers, LeBron James have mutual interest in reunion
item.description: Scoop B. Robinson: The Cleveland Cavaliers and LeBron James do have mutual interest in a return. And with that comes what money would look like. The Los Angeles Lakers could offer him more money on paper than the Cavs can, but I know that the Cavs and some members on their roster have had conversations with LeBron about a potential pairing and that it's just been more of a listening and a conversation. I will say that as much as people believe that the Cavs and the Lakers are the only team, I wi

item.title: Mike Gansey, Nick U'Ren among candidates for Philadelphia 76ers front-office position
item.description: Marc Stein: Cavaliers GM Mike Gansey and Phoenix Mercury GM Nick U'ren, who worked for the Warriors before his move to the WNBA, have emerged as names to watch in Philadelphia's front office search, @TheSteinLine has learned. Adding to our existing Philly coverage: tinyurl.com/2hw5e8pj

item.title: LeBron wanted Rich Paul, Maverick, Randy, and Lynn …
item.description: Brendan Haywood: "I tell you another thing in Miami that was a microaggression that Pat Riley did. LeBron, I didn't realize it at the time until I played with LeBron in Cleveland. I was like, oh, that's what happened. LeBron wanted Rich Paul, Maverick, Randy, and Lynn Merritt. He wanted their season tickets to be right beside the Heat bench. Oh, yeah. And you remember where were their tickets? They were right beside the bench, but it was the other team's bench. See, these are the little petty th

item.title: “We have to put pressure on them in all phases, …
item.description: But, even with the importance of Mitchell and Harden to the Cavs’ offense, Brown said the Knicks have to be “conscious of trying to play the right way against this group.” “We have to put pressure on them in all phases, starting with transition and ending with the ability to try and get an offensive rebound,” he said. “You wanna make all of their guys, especially their guys that playmake for them and do a lot for them, you wanna make them work as much as you can.

item.description: Ian Begley: Knicks are listing OG Anunoby (hamstring strain) as probable for Game 1 of the ECF tomorrow:

There were conversations, some teams more than other teams. You think about teams like Miami and Minnesota, those were more extensive conversations. Those were more back-and-forth engagement conversations. Then you have teams like Cleveland and the Lakers and the Celtics that checked and the Knicks that checked in, that had interest. There were some con

item.title: The network will begin broadcasting the series between …
item.description: ESPN’s plans for Inside the NBA for the Eastern Conference Finals have come into focus. The network will begin broadcasting the series between the Knicks and Cavaliers on Tuesday night. An ESPN spokesperson confirmed to Front Office Sports that the plans for the iconic studio show, which it is licensing from TNT, are as follows: Every ECF game will have a one-hour pregame show, except for Game 3 on Sat. May 23, which is on ABC and will have a half-hour pregame. Halftime of every game Postgame sh

item.title: Jarrett Allen on 'lights were too bright' comment from 2023: I'm a new person now, I've grown and evolved
item.description: Dave McMenamin: “That comment was that comment. What I said is what it is. I’m a new person now. I’ve grown, evolved, have a lot more playoff series under my belt, a lot more games under my belt …” - Jarrett Allen on his “lights are brighter than expected” at MSG quote during the 2023 playoffs

item.title: “This is a business trip. This isn’t something where …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: “This is a business trip. This isn’t something where I’m here to be like, ‘Man, I’m so excited to be home.’ I did that already. And we lost.” - Westchester County, NY native Donovan Mitchell on the Cavs’ second playoff series versus his hometown Knicks in the last four years

item.title: There are different things that we can do. We’ve got a …
item.description: Donovan Mitchell: Guarding Jalen Brunson, man, it’s by committee. He can get down there [in the paint], he can manipulate the game, he can get to his spots, knock down shots. We got to make it tough on him and understand that obviously, he’s the head of the snake. No one guy can stop him. It’s going to be a group effort. There are different things that we can do. We’ve got a lot of respect for him and that team, and as a group we have to be locked in.

item.title: For myself and James [Harden], I don’t want to give …
item.description: Donovan Mitchell: For myself and James [Harden], I don’t want to give too much to the media as far as narratives, because whether we won or lost this game [Sunday], I don’t think this describes either one of us as players and how talented we are and what we’ve been able to do in our career. But you also understand that there is the reality of a narrative and that it’s something. There’s a reason why he chose to come here. For myself and him, the job is not done. It’s great. Smiles. Fantastic. Bu

item.description: Krishna Narsu: From GeniusIQ, On Catch and Shoot 3s: CLE: 77 fga, 54.42 qsq, 55.86 qsp, 4 open NYK: 93 fga, 53.79 qsq, 54.01 qsp, 11 open CLE generated better quality looks but took less C&S 3s.

item.title: "We know they have a massive rest advantage. We know …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Kenny Atkinson a rare coach who admits he reads the media stories. Said he knows most are picking Knicks. "We know they have a massive rest advantage. We know they're massively favored in the series. From our perspective we've got to halt their momentum. That starts tonight."

item.title: OG Anunoby will play in Game 1
item.description: Ian Begley: OG Anunoby will play tonight in Game 1 of ECF, Knicks say. He is starting as well.

item.title: Jalen Brunson leads rally as Knicks storm back to take Game 1
item.description: Fred Katz: FINAL: Knicks 115, Cavaliers 104 (OT). Knicks come back from down 22 in fourth quarter. • Brunson 38-5-6 • Bridges 18 & 5 • Towns 13-13-5 • Hart 13-7-4 Knicks lead the series 1-0.

item.title: Knicks come back from 22 in the fourth quarter, their …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks come back from 22 in the fourth quarter, their largest comeback win in the playoffs in franchise history. It's the fourth time they've comeback from down 20 or more over last 2 postseasons.

item.title: Knicks just closed Game 1 on a 44-11 (!!) run against …
item.description: Legion Hoops: The Knicks just closed Game 1 on a 44-11 (!!) run against the Cavs. Unreal.

item.title: James Harden: 15 points, six turnover, 5-16 from the …
item.description: Chris Mannix: James Harden: 15 points, six turnover, 5-16 from the floor, 1-8 from three. The Cavs survived a rough Harden shooting night in Game 7 against Detroit. They lose Game 1 in New York.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell appeared to tweak something in his …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Donovan Mitchell appeared to tweak something in his leg in the fourth quarter and he was uninvolved the rest of the way. Looked to be limping back to the locker room.

item.title: Kenny Atkinson on thoughts of benching James Harden: No, he's been one of our best defenders in the playoffs, I trust him
item.description: Michael Scotto: Kenny Atkinson was asked if he thought of benching James Harden on defensive possessions at the end of Game 1. He replied, “No. He’s been one of our best defenders in these playoffs. I trust him. Smart. Great hands. Didn’t think about that.”

item.title: Mike Brown called Landry Shamet ‘the difference in the …
item.description: Ian Begley: Mike Brown called Landry Shamet ‘the difference in the ballgame on both ends of the floor” in Knicks’ 22-point 4th quarter comeback in Game 1.

item.title: "It's 1 game. We could have lost by 40.
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Donovan Mitchell: "It's 1 game. We could have lost by 40. Still would have been 1-0"

item.title: "We lost, we fucking blew it"
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Donovan Mitchell on the message he gave in the locker room: "We lost, we fucking blew it"

item.title: James Harden finished with more turnovers (6) than …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: James Harden finished with more turnovers (6) than made field goals (5) for the 32nd time in his playoff career & the 6th time this postseason.

item.title: Kenny Atkinson on Cavs collapse: 'We got a little unlucky'
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Kenny Atkinson: "We got a little unlucky...they dominated us in the 4th quarter"

item.title: "I like to hold my timeouts. I try to hold them"

item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Kenny Atkinson: "We weren't great defensively in the 4th quarter. The ball stuck a little bit too much. That was the key"

item.title: Cavs gave up 28 points off turnovers tonight, the 6th …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Cavs gave up 28 points off turnovers tonight, the 6th time they've allowed 25 or more points off TOs this postseason. That's the most such games by a team in single postseason over the last 30 years.

item.title: NBA teams were 3-747 when down by 20 or more in the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: NBA teams were 3-747 when down by 20 or more in the 4th quarter of a playoff game over the last 30 years. They are now 4-747. Knicks come back from 22 in the fourth quarter, the second-largest 4th quarter comeback in the playoffs in the play-by-play era (since 1997).

item.title: Largest fourth quarter comeback playoffs wins in the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Largest fourth quarter comeback playoffs wins in the play-by-play era: Clippers, down 24 vs MEM in 2012 Knicks, down 22 vs CLE tonight Celtics, down 21 vs NJN in 2002 Warriors, down 20 vs NOP in 2015

item.title: Last night, the Knicks targeted James Harden with 27 …
item.description: ‪Zach Kram‬: Last night, the Knicks targeted James Harden with 27 picks. That's the most picks he's ever defended in any game in his career (in the tracking era, since 2013-14). The Knicks scored 1.53 points per pick (!) that led directly to a shot, foul, or shot one pass away.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell on Cleveland Cavaliers ownership: …
item.description: Donovan Mitchell on Cleveland Cavaliers ownership: “It’s a big reason why I re-signed. Look at the 25 buses that was sent to Detroit, that’s a small thing that we done to show they’re about winning. This is something I’ve never seen before. It’s not like this everywhere.”

item.title: Mike Brown: 'It’s no secret we were attacking James Harden'
item.description: ‪Michael Scotto‬: Knicks coach Mike Brown on Jalen Brunson being guarded by James Harden: “It’s no secret we were attacking (James) Harden.”

item.title: “Hey @cavs fans, that’s as tough as it gets. Hard to …
item.description: Gilbert, however, maintained his optimism after their bitter defeat. “Hey @cavs fans, that’s as tough as it gets. Hard to find anything good to say about blowing a 22-point lead in the fourth quarter on the road in the ECF. But at the end of the day, it’s one game, and this team has proven over and over again that it can come back stronger than ever,” said Gilbert. “Let’s get Game 2 Thursday night, and all of this will soon be a distant memory.”

item.title: Donovan Mitchell and James Harden have the most clutch …
item.description: ‪Jason Patt‬: Donovan Mitchell and James Harden have the most clutch FGA in these playoffs. The shooting percentages are not pretty.

item.title: Sam Merrill off 129 oRTG, 100 dRTG
item.description: Mike Zavagno: Sam Merrill on 86 oRTG, 137 dRTG -17 Sam Merrill off 129 oRTG, 100 dRTG +6

item.title: Kenny Atkinson on Knicks saying they were targeting James Harden: It was mostly our team defense
item.description: Chris Fedor: #Cavs Kenny Atkinson on the Knicks openly saying they were targeting James Harden

item.title: Draymond Green on Knicks targeting James Harden on defense: This isn't just a coaching issue, you gotta have guys that want that challenge
item.description: Draymond Green: "Landry Shamet and Mikal Bridges hit some clutch threes in the fourth and overtime. Jalen Brunson got going in the fourth quarter and went ballistic. What wasn't expected was the way it happened: Jalen Brunson living on an island with James Harden guarding him. That's just not something you can let happen. James Harden's been at it for 17 years; teams have tried this over and over again. You have to expect this is what they're ultimately going to go to. You have to have some othe

item.title: Josh Hart on not being in game during closing minutes: I put the success of the team over my success anyday
item.description: Kristian Winfield: Josh Hart says he wants to be out there helping the team but he doesn’t have an ego and if that means watching a game from the bench in crunch time late, so be it. “I put the success of the team over the success of myself any day.”

item.title: Josh Hart says he’s expecting the Cavs to leave him …
item.description: Kristian Winfield: Josh Hart says he’s expecting the Cavs to leave him open again in Game 2: “Yeah it’s probably the same game plan. They were up 20. They’re probably gonna do the same thing and I’m gonna shoot the exact same shots.”

item.title: They’re gonna be ready, they’re gonna mix it up, and …
item.description: Kristian Winfield: Mike Brown on potential Cavs adjustments: “We know they’re gonna come out ready to play. They came to New York to win one game, and it’s still within reach… they’re gonna be ready, they’re gonna mix it up, and it’s our job to make sure we’re more efficient and more diverse offensively.”

item.title: Mike Brown suffered a calf injury at the end of Game 1 …
item.description: Kristian Winfield: Mike Brown suffered a calf injury at the end of Game 1 and says he’s good to go for Game 2

item.title: “When I was in Golden State and we played Houston, we …
item.description: “When I was in Golden State and we played Houston, we counted James Harden’s dribbles,” Brown said. “We told our guys he’s dribbling close to 1,000 times a game. Keep picking him up full court and make him dribble. At the end of the game, it would wear him down.” When that happened, the Knicks exposed Harden defensively with Jalen Brunson dominating him down the stretch during the shocking rally.

item.title: Stephon Castle joins Sleepy Floyd, Cade Cunningham, …
item.description: Justin Russo: Stephon Castle joins Sleepy Floyd, Cade Cunningham, and James Harden as the only players in league history to have 20 turnovers during any 2-game stretch. Castle the first to do it in the postseason.

item.title: One of the most notable, current Cleveland Cavaliers …
item.description: As Bob Myers, president of sports for Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment, leads the search, several candidates have emerged. One of the most notable, current Cleveland Cavaliers general manager Mike Gansey, has been an executive in Cleveland for almost a decade and is considered a strong candidate to take over for Morey, according to league sources. He was once a standout guard for a West Virginia team that surprisingly went all the way to the Elite Eight of the NCAA Tournament in 2006.

item.title: James Harden, Cavaliers interested in an extension, but his performance in Knicks series could affect his contract
item.description: How James Harden performs the rest of the series could affect his contract this summer. He has a player option for next season worth $42.3 million, but he was acquired with both the Cavs and Harden's camp interested in an extension, sources told ESPN.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell on Cleveland: I've grown fond of this organization because it feels peaceful, even through stressful times
item.description: The remaining goals for Altman, who is on his third extension since becoming GM and signed a multiyear deal after last season, sources told ESPN, is to build sustainable success and win championships -- without relying on or planning for a third stint with James to make that happen. "I've grown very fond of this organization because I've seen the steps toward winning both on and off the court and then it just feels peaceful," Mitchell told ESPN. "It feels connected even through stressful times,

item.title: Sources told ESPN that last season's loss to the …
item.description: Sources told ESPN that last season's loss to the Indiana Pacers, spoiling a 64-win season, was devastating, with one saying it felt like "getting punched in the face." It was then that they knew they needed that infusion.

item.title: "When LeBron left, we just fell flat on our faces …
item.description: THE CAVS WENT through an organizational overhaul in 2018 when James left for a second time as a free agent. "When LeBron left, we just fell flat on our faces because we just weren't rooted in anything," a team source told ESPN. "We weren't rooted in anything foundational in terms of culture or team-building or player development. "We were just rooted in the culture of LeBron."

item.title: It took some time to settle in. Since Altman took over …
item.description: What followed were three painful seasons, going 60-159, before the climb began. Altman, whose first year as general manager coincided with James' last with the franchise, used the time to cement his philosophy for team-building, sources told ESPN. The Cavs would win again through player development, and through the collective spirit of finding players who looked at Cleveland as a preferred destination. It took some time to settle in. Since Altman took over for his mentor, former Cavs GM David Gr

item.title: "Four years without him, in the rebuild: one play-in …
item.description: Altman pounced, putting together a trade to send Markkanen, Sexton and three unprotected first-round picks to land Mitchell. And Cleveland took off from there. "Four years without him, in the rebuild: one play-in game," a team source said of Mitchell. "Four years with him: four playoff appearances, three second-round appearances, a conference finals appearance."

item.title: “The first one was on his optic nerve, between his eye …
item.description: There are several titles that Dan Gilbert can go by: entrepreneur, philanthropist, billionaire, NBA team owner. But of all the titles, the one Gilbert’s most proud of is that of father. Gilbert, who is the Chairman of the Board of Rocket Companies and is the majority owner of the Cleveland Cavaliers, has five children. His oldest son, Nick, died in 2023 after a long battle with neurofibromatosis, or NF, a rare disease that causes tumors to grow on nerve tissues. NF is one of the most prevalent r

item.title: On his 10th birthday, Nick learned that he would soon …
item.description: That tumor led to Nick’s first bout of chemotherapy. But it would not be his last. On his 10th birthday, Nick learned that he would soon need to undergo his first major surgery. Gilbert said his son responded to the difficult news as he did to all challenges in life, with optimism and a sense of humor. ″[Nick] just wanted to know if there was macaroni in the hospital,” Gilbert said. He added, “He always believed that he was going to get through all this. And so did we.”

item.title: “There were some tumors, that just kind of got away …
item.description: After the surgery, Nick underwent several rounds of chemo and radiation therapy. But he didn’t let that stand in the way of achieving his goals. Despite his battle with NF, Nick was still able to maintain close friendships, attend Michigan State, and even work as an intern in the business development unit at his dad’s company. But by 2018, the disease was catching up with him. “There were some tumors, that just kind of got away from us,” Gilbert said. “We had two major surgeries that year. And f

item.title: “He was loved by everybody. Everybody that met him …
item.description: For Gilbert, who considered his son his best friend, losing Nick was a devastating blow. And he was not alone in his grief. Before he died, Nick had become a symbol of preservation and determination in two cities: his hometown of Detroit, and in Cleveland, where he had become a fixture at Cavs games. “He was loved by everybody. Everybody that met him loved him. He loved everybody.” Gilbert said it was frustrating, having billions of dollars and access to the best doctors in the world, but still

item.title: “We raised $12 million in one night. We keep breaking …
item.description: Something that Gilbert and his family have worked hard to control is how Nick is remembered. They’ve taken several steps to ensure that Nick’s memory will live on. They opened Gilly’s Clubhouse in downtown Detroit. Gilly was a nickname given to Nick by his friends. The upscale sports bar was Nick’s vision and a project that he was developing before he died. Gilbert’s other children, Nick’s siblings, started The Gilly Project, a community based non-profit dedicated to Nick’s honor. And Gilbert ma

item.title: Kenny Atkinson was content with quality of shots the …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Kenny Atkinson was content with quality of shots the Knicks got at the end of Game 1 but he also wrestled with process vs. results for Game 2. "The real dilemma in coaching," he said. Asked him if he'd keep the same coverages on Jalen Brunson. TBD. "Everybody can wait and see, and then criticize after."

item.title: Mike Brown was asked about his approach for Game 2 …
item.description: Ian Begley: Mike Brown was asked about his approach for Game 2 after Knicks’ comeback Game 1 win: “I think our guys understand that we got away with one (in G1). And we don’t want to let that happen again. Because Cleveland’s a great team … and they’re not going to let that happen again.”

item.title: Kenny Atkinson on Jaylon Tyson's lack of playing time: …
item.description: Chris Fedor: #Cavs Kenny Atkinson on Jaylon Tyson's lack of playing time: "Definitely still in the cards. He's still alive. We'll need him. Don't know if it's tonight, but he's right there. We were playing well with the group we had out there most of the game. He's in the bullpen warming up"

item.title: Knicks win ninth consecutive playoff game in Josh Hart's big night
item.description: Ryan Field: Knicks win Game 2 to take 2-0 series lead. 109-93 the happy final behind a playoff career-high 26 points from Josh Hart. Game 3: Saturday at 8 on ABC!

item.title: The Cavaliers went scoreless for 332 seconds in the …
item.description: Law Murray: In a game decided by 16 points. The Cavaliers went scoreless for 332 seconds in the third quarter, and the Knicks turned a tie game into an 18-point lead. 5.5 minutes that decided Game 2.

item.title: James Harden passes Kobe Bryant in all-time playoff steals
item.description: NBA: Congrats to James Harden of the Cavaliers for moving up to 6th on the all-time playoff STEALS list! NBA Playoffs presented by Google

item.title: Donovan Mitchell compares Josh Hart's impact to Alex Caruso
item.description: They played the math on Josh Hart, and even though he isn't a huge fan of analytics, it was to his advantage on this night. "Sometimes you got to tip your cap," Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell said. "Not comparing the players, but you see a similar situation in the other series. And with [Alex] Caruso [Oklahoma City Thunder guard], they're guarding him kind of the same way. So I'm not saying he's him or whatever or vice versa, but you just got to adjust, and we'll look at the film and figure ou

item.title: Hart said his “feel for the game” helped him in a big …
item.description: Josh Hart redeemed himself for his Game 1 blunders by being the main offensive factor for the Knicks in their Game 2 victory over the Cavaliers. Hart put up 26 points (a playoff career high), seven assists, four rebounds and two steals in 10-of-21 shooting from the field and 5-for-11 from beyond the arc in the Knicks’ 109-93 win. Hart was benched down the stretch during the Knicks’ overtime 115-114 Game 1 win over the Cavaliers on Tuesday, posting a team-worst minus-23 rating in 30 minutes. Hart

item.title: "It's just who Josh is. He's a gamer. He knew what he …
item.description: Coach Mike Brown correctly pulled Hart in Game 1 for Landry Shamet. But having Hart on the court causes so much confusion for the Cavaliers as they try to crossmatch, leaving an unbalanced floor and the energetic Hart in position to take full advantage with open shots and setting up teammates. When Hart wasn't finding teammates, it was Brunson finding Hart for one of his playoff-high 14 assists. "It's just who Josh is. He's a gamer. He knew what he had to do in terms of adjustments he needed to

item.title: Cleveland shot 9 for 26 (26%) from beyond the arc …
item.description: Cleveland shot 9 for 26 (26%) from beyond the arc Thursday, including 6 for 20 on open looks, according to ESPN Research. Sam Merrill, who is shooting 40% from 3 in the playoffs, went 0-for-7 from 3; he had never attempted more than five 3s in any game, regular season or playoffs, without making at least one. "We just hit no shots," Cavs guard James Harden said. "We had a lot of open shots. Make shots, hit a couple of those, win the ball game. Throughout the entire game, our process was right. I

item.title: The Cavs were held under 100 points for the fourth …
item.description: The Cavs were held under 100 points for the fourth time this postseason, something that happened only four times during the regular season. "We're in the same place as last round," Cavs forward Evan Mobley said. "So, the good thing is we've been here before. It's nothing that we haven't fought through and prevailed. So, we just got to win these next two at home and keep it moving."

item.title: "We're not tired. We're not tired. We're ready to go …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Donovan Mitchell: "We're not tired. We're not tired. We're ready to go for game 3"

item.title: "I'm great," he said with a smirk. "I'm great. I'm …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Donovan Mitchell says he is fine and healthy. Kenny Atkinson had said he was working through something. Richard Jefferson and Tim Legler said during the game they felt he didn't have his usual explosiveness. He disagrees. "I'm great," he said with a smirk. "I'm great. I'm great"

item.title: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani quietly attended …
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani quietly attended the Knicks’ Game 2 win over the Cavaliers at Madison Square Garden on Thursday night — paying face value for tickets near a section that boasted $700 seats. Mamdani, who did not list his attendance on his public schedule, posted a smiling photo alongside Public Advocate Jumaane Williams inside the Garden with the caption “Knicks forever” — only after the team’s astonishing 109-93 Game 2 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference

item.title: Can the Lakers afford to bring back LeBron James on a …
item.description: Can the Lakers afford to bring back LeBron James on a $50 million salary and still be in a position to bolster their roster. When factoring in a new contract for Austin Reaves, that answer is a resounding no. Would James compromise to return to L.A.? A $30 million salary would allow the Lakers to re-sign Luke Kennard, Rui Hachimura and use the full $15 million non-tax midlevel exception. The Lakers could also act as a cap space team but without Hachimura and Kennard. They would then have $20 mil

item.title: Bulls considering Cavs' Johnnie Bryant for their head coaching position
item.description: Furthermore, Cleveland Cavaliers associate head coach Johnnie Bryant is a potential candidate being considered for Chicago’s head coaching position, league sources told HoopsHype. Bryant was a finalist for Phoenix’s head coaching job before Jordan Ott was ultimately hired.

item.title: Portland also called the Cleveland Cavaliers for …
item.description: Portland also called the Cleveland Cavaliers for permission to speak with assistant coach Omar Cook early in the process, which was believed to be for a possible assistant coaching position, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: 76ers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson has also …
item.description: Thus far, external candidates, including Cleveland’s Mike Gansey, Minnesota’s Matt Lloyd, and Phoenix Mercury general manager Nick U'Ren, have emerged. Internally, 76ers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson has also positioned himself well to remain with the organization and be in the mix for a possible promotion, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: As for the Cavaliers, their partnership with The …
item.description: Since the 2023-24 season, Sotheby has worked with the league in hosting live NBA auctions (New York, Paris) and activations (Abu Dhabi). Sotheby’s sold Cooper Flagg’s debut jersey in February for a record-setting $1 million, which the league said eclipsed Victor Wembanyama’s previous record when his jersey sold for $762,000 in 2023. In March, Sotheby auctioned off various Scottie Pippen’s autographed memorabilia from the 1992 Olympics and from parts of his six NBA Finals appearances. The league

item.title: Another fan paid $1,505 for Max Strus’ city edition …
item.description: The Cavs and the Realest also auctioned off a Lakers bench seat from their game on Jan. 28 for $1,905. A game-used rim and net from that same game sold for $2,305. Another fan paid $1,505 for Max Strus’ city edition game-worn warmup pullover ahead of the Cavs’ game against the Orlando Magic on March 24. And yet… “We don’t want to sell only $10,000 items,” Keeney said. “That’s not accessible for the kid that’s growing up. We need to onboard a new generation and demographic of collectors.” Hence,

item.title: 'Let's go Knicks' chant heard in Cleveland ahead of Game 3
item.description: Stefan Bondy: A Let's Go Knicks chant has already started in Cleveland.

item.title: There is a pretty good amount of blue and orange here …
item.description: Chris Fedor: There is a pretty good amount of blue and orange here inside Rocket Arena -- and it's not #Cavs fans wearing their throwbacks from the 80s and early 90s.

item.title: Kenny Atkinson played it coy pregame, saying "We'll …
item.description: Chris Fedor: #Cavs coach Kenny Atkinson played it coy pregame, saying "We'll see," when asked about a potential lineup change. But Cleveland was never making one for tonight's game. The decision was made well before tonight to stay with Dean Wade as the fifth starter.

item.title: Kenny Atkinson talked to Donovan Mitchell, who relayed …
item.description: Ian Begley: Kenny Atkinson talked to Donovan Mitchell, who relayed that he’s dealing with ‘normal bumps and bruises…wear and tear.’ No formal injury. Just normal soreness/discomfort for any player who has played as often as Mitchell has during postseason.

item.title: Jalen Brunson takes Knicks one win away from the NBA Finals
item.description: Bob Finnan: Final: Knicks 121, Cavs 108. Jalen Brunson has game-high 30 points for Knicks, who have a 3-0 lead in the best-of-seven series.

item.title: Cavs have 'no interest' in trading Evan Mobley for Giannis Antetokounmpo as of now
item.description: The Cavs, probably soon, are going home for the summer with the most expensive roster in NBA history and no finals berth to show for it. The rules are written such that it is unlikely some key role players will be back, and it is nearly impossible to bring in better replacements without first making significant moves to shed salary. Otherwise, you’re looking at something drastic like trading Evan Mobley for Giannis Antetokounmpo — though two league sources say Cleveland has shown no interest in

item.title: Cavs have been outscored in almost every hustle stat …
item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: Cavs have been outscored in almost every hustle stat this postseason. -123 in fast break points -72 in points off turnovers +4 in second chance points

item.title: “They came right out of the gate running it on us,” …
item.description: “They came right out of the gate running it on us,” Atkinson said after the game. “You know, Mike said it. Listen, there’s no big mystery. Our guys have played 50% more minutes than them. If I’m the opposing coach, I’m like, ‘Man, get these guys, run these guys, wear them out, be super physical.’ It’s a good strategy.”

item.title: “I did think their physicality and energy level was …
item.description: “I did think their physicality and energy level was much higher than ours,” Atkinson said. “Kind of just call it what it is [...] they were the more physical team tonight. I think their physicality and energy, we just couldn’t get to that level to combat it.”

item.title: Donovan Mitchell on Knicks fans in Cleveland: "I'm from New York. This doesn't shock me"
item.description: Donovan Mitchell on Knicks fans in Cleveland: "I'm from New York. This doesn't shock me. They do it in every arena. It's like Cowboys fans, just who they are. I don't think it's a Cleveland thing. It's how Knicks fans are. I was one back in the day. Cleveland has the best fans in the world. I stand on that"

item.title: Jaylen Brown, Kawhi Leonard, Jalen Brunson earn All-NBA Second Team honors
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia All-NBA Second Team: Jaylen Brown, @celtics Jalen Brunson, @nyknicks Kevin Durant, @HoustonRockets Kawhi Leonard, @LAClippers Donovan Mitchell, @cavs

item.title: "I think analytically, I think we've won the -- I said …
item.description: But Cavs coach Kenny Atkinson thinks his team still has a chance against the New York Knicks, citing analytical data from the series' first three games. In fact, according to the numbers, the Cavs should be winning the series, Atkinson said. "I think analytically, I think we've won the -- I said three out of three [games in the series], we're two out of three in the expected wins," Atkinson told reporters Sunday. "I don't know if you guys follow that -- the expected score. We've won two out of t

item.title: "If you believe in process and all that ... take that …
item.description: The Cavs shot 50% (42-of-84) from the floor Saturday but struggled from long range, going just 12-of-41 (29%) from 3-point range. Cleveland missed 10 of 17 open 3-point shots in Game 3, according to ESPN Research, and is 21 for 65 (32%) on such shots in the series. The Knicks, on the other hand, have made 42% of their open 3-pointers in the series, according to ESPN Research. "If you believe in process and all that ... take that layer," Atkinson said. "I know I don't throw that on them [the play

item.title: “The belief. You can tell when there’s lip service to …
item.description: Ian Begley: Kenny Atkinson was asked what stood out to him during Cavs’ film session yesterday: “The belief. You can tell when there’s lip service to belief. I think there’s real belief with this group.” Atkinson adds: “We all know one win can change things….The Knicks are on a roll… but it would be great to put some doubt in there mind (by winning Game 4).”

item.title: If the Cavaliers lose in 4 or 5, they'll become the …
item.description: Zach Kram: If the Cavaliers lose in 4 or 5, they'll become the 4th conference finalist this century with a losing playoff record. But the silver lining is what happened with the 3 previous teams: - '24 Pacers—made the finals in '25 - '20 Nuggets—won in '23 - '03 Pistons—won in '04

item.title: Schroder is out
item.description: Danny Cunningham: Schroder is out.

item.title: Knicks fans banned from Cavs courtside seats
item.description: At least 10 other Knicks fans with courtside seats had been called by a Cavaliers executive and told they no longer had seats, a league source told The Post. After those fans were informed they no longer had courtside seats, the Cavs did relocate them to a higher section inside the arena for Game 4.

item.title: Fat Joe appeared on ESPN’s “NBA Today” live in …
item.description: Fat Joe appeared on ESPN’s “NBA Today” live in Cleveland when he said, unprompted, that the Cavs took the tickets from him because they didn’t want Knicks fans sitting that close. “We had bought some courtside tickets to the game and once they found out it was superfan Fat Joe, they was like, I can’t sit courtside. New York Knick fans can’t sit courtside,” he said on the program. “They took the tickets away courtside after we purchased it, so shame on you all.”

item.title: How quickly it took the Knicks to build a 20-point …
item.description: Zach Kram: How quickly it took the Knicks to build a 20-point lead in potential closeout games this spring: vs. Atlanta: 11 minutes vs. Philly: 11 minutes vs. Cleveland: 13.5 minutes

item.title: James Harden finished with more turnovers (5) than …
item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: James Harden finished with more turnovers (5) than made field goals (2) for the 33rd time in his playoff career & the 7th time this postseason.

item.title: If the Cleveland Cavaliers want a shred of hope, the …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: If the Cleveland Cavaliers want a shred of hope, the Knicks are gonna be the fourth straight Eastern Conference finals loser to make the Finals the next year. They follow the Pacers, Celtics and Heat.

item.title: Knicks return to the NBA Finals 27 years later
item.description: Fred Katz‬: FINAL: Knicks 130, Cavaliers 93. Knicks win the series 4-0 and are heading to the NBA Finals. • Towns 19-14-3 • Brunson 15-2-5 • Bridges 15-3-5 • Anunoby 17-7-4

item.title: The Cavs have allowed 20+ points off turnovers in 10 …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Some analytics: the Cavs have allowed 20+ points off turnovers in 10 games this postseason. That's the most in one playoff run since at least 1997.

item.title: James Harden is finishing the season as the player …
item.description: HoopsHype: James Harden is finishing the season as the player with the most playoff wins without an NBA title. Tied at 98 with Karl Malone.

item.title: This is the furthest the Cavs went without LeBron …
item.description: ‪Danny Cunningham‬: #Cavs lose to the Knicks 130-93 to finish off a sweep in the Eastern Conference Finals. This is the furthest the Cavs went without LeBron since 1992, but the cost of getting there made the end result an embarrassing series. An interesting offseason lies ahead.

item.title: James Harden is losing this record tonight
item.description: HoopsHype: James Harden is losing this record tonight.

item.description: NBACentral: “Genuinely I do feel like we're the better team" - James Harden

item.description: ‪Michael Scotto‬: James Harden on if he wants to remain with the Cavs and if he expects to stay: “Yes, 100 percent. Definitely to both. I think we found something. It’s tough not ending how we wanted to, but I think we found something.”

item.title: Donovan Mitchell: 'I love it here'
item.description: Marc Stein: Cleveland's Donovan Mitchell at tonight's postgame press conference: "I love it here. I don't know any other way to say it."

item.title: Donovan Mitchell on James Harden: "I hope he is back.
item.description: Chris Fedor: #Cavs Donovan Mitchell on James Harden: "I hope he is back. He helped myself and this group get somewhere we have never been"

item.description: Marc J. Spears: James Harden says “100 percent” that he wants to be back in Cleveland and expects to be back with the Cavs.

item.title: Kenny Atkinson on the Cavs: “We took a step. This is …
item.description: ‪Michael Scotto‬: Kenny Atkinson on the Cavs: “We took a step. This is kind of how this works. You have to keep climbing. We are going, obviously, in a positive direction. Fell short. There’s no bigger motivator to get to the next level.”

item.title: Atkinson on if this sweep erases goodwill from winning …
item.description: Danny Cunningham: Atkinson on if this sweep erases goodwill from winning Game 7 in Detroit: "Listen, you're disappointed. Everyone is disappointed."

item.title: Kenny Atkinson: 'I have confidence in myself, confidence in the group'
item.description: Danny Cunningham‬: Kenny Atkinson on potential changes: "I have confidence in myself, confidence in the group, the roster talk, that's for down the line. Our front office has done a phenomenal job...obviously there will be decisions to be made, like every summer."

item.title: James Harden is now 12-17 in elimination games in his …
item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: James Harden is now 12-17 in elimination games in his career. He's shot 35% or worse from the field in 15 of his 28 career elimination games.

item.description: Jackson Flickinger: James Harden got subbed out for three minutes, and the Cavs immediately gave up a 13-5 run. This has been the case all playoffs. The Cavs have a +1.8 net rating. When he's off, they're -8.8. That's a difference of +10.6

item.title: Their three largest playoff series margins of victory …
item.description: ‪Law Murray 📘‬: The New York Knicks have been a National Basketball Association team from the beginning of the damn league Their three largest playoff series margins of victory have been this year +105 vs Hawks +89 vs 76ers +77 vs Cavaliers previous high was +54 in 1972 East semis vs Bullets.

item.title: Knicks bench outscored the Cavs 58-24 tonight, their …
item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: Knicks bench outscored the Cavs 58-24 tonight, their largest differential in any game this postseason. They've won the bench point differential in 8 of 14 games over this run, after losing it in every game last playoffs.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell deflects on LeBron James return question
item.description: Danny Cunningham: Donovan Mitchell on if he wants LeBron James to return: "That is not for me. I am not trying to get a headline. That's a Koby Altman question, that's a Mike Gansey question."

item.title: Jalen Brunson: 'The belief that the organization has in me has been amazing'
item.description: In his fourth postseason as a Knick, Brunson led the franchise back to the NBA Finals for the first time in 27 years, capped by their emphatic series sweep of the Cavaliers on Monday night. “The belief that the organization has in me has been amazing, and something I don’t take for granted, and something not a lot of people get the opportunity to do,” Brunson said after notching 15 points and five assists in the 130-93 clincher over the Cavaliers in Game 4. “So I’m very thankful. It’s an honor t

item.title: "I know for a fact he's from Long Island, he don't …
item.description: NBA head coaches, at times, receive the brunt of the blame regardless of the context that led them there. "I know for a fact he's from Long Island, he don't give a damn [about the outside criticism]," Mitchell said, as he and Atkinson have New York ties. "But it's just the world we live in. We could have made it to the Finals and got swept, somebody was going to say something. I told you out there, even if we won, there's going to be something. 'Can we do it?' It's always going to be something f

item.title: "Ultimate players coach. Like, he gets it," Harden …
item.description: Mitchell certainly endorsed Atkinson after the Game 4 loss. Harden, too, echoed that sentiment, and put the conference finals loss more on the Cavs' shooting woes than anything else. "Ultimate players coach. Like, he gets it," Harden said. "He understands the team. And, yeah, of course, somebody's gonna have to take the criticism, whether it's myself or Kenny, the entire team, whoever. They're gonna put it on somebody. But I think for Kenny, he did an unbelievable job of getting me acclimated as

item.title: "James Harden is going to be a Cav next year. ... That …
item.description: Get Up: "James Harden is going to be a Cav next year. ... That new deal will probably be structured in a way that will allow the Cavs to dip below the second apron." @Windhorstespn gives some insight on the Cavs as they prepare for the offseason 🏀
